skill.name: capa-officer
skill.description: Senior CAPA Officer specialist for managing Corrective and Preventive Actions within Quality Management Systems. Provides CAPA process management, root cause analysis, effectiveness verification, and continuous improvement coordination. Use for CAPA investigations, corrective action planning, preventive action implementation, and CAPA system optimization.
skill.summary: Guides CAPA investigations, root cause analysis, action planning, and effectiveness verification.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and appear to be false positives from documentation text, Markdown code fences, and backticked file names. No prompt injection, malicious command execution, credential access, or network behavior was found. A benign executable placeholder script is present, so the skill carries a low script risk factor.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hits are substrings inside normal CAPA descriptions, placeholder asset examples, and reference documentation headings. No cryptographic function, key handling, encryption workflow, or use of DES or MD5 was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose or placeholder headings, not cryptographic code. The surrounding files do not define encryption operations or algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Static Matches Are Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown formatting and a fenced workflow diagram. Resource names are documented, but the skill does not instruct the model to execute shell commands from those backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks delimit Markdown blocks and file names only. No shell command, Ruby expression, command substitution, or user input execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Static Match Is Plain Text
low_findings.description: The env_access hit points to CAPA source categorization in a trend analysis checklist. It does not reference environment variables, configuration libraries, secrets, or process environment access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a quality-management data category item. There is no process.env, os.environ, dotenv, or equivalent configuration access.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Static Match Is Plain Text
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance hit points to a training quality-system touchpoint. No network scanning command, host probing, port enumeration, or external connection logic appears at the cited location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line discusses training competency requirements only. The nearby section is quality-system documentation, not network tooling or reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Benign Placeholder Script Present
low_findings.description: The skill includes an executable Python placeholder script that only prints a message. This is not malicious, but executable files require normal marketplace caution because future changes could add behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script content is directly visible and only prints a static message. The low risk comes from executable capability, not from current harmful behavior.

content.user_title: Manage CAPA Investigations and Action Plans
content.value_statement: CAPA work requires consistent investigation structure, action tracking, and evidence-based closure. This skill helps teams plan investigations, analyze root causes, and verify corrective actions.
actual_capabilities: Structures CAPA investigations from initiation through closure.
Suggests root cause analysis methods for quality, process, and safety issues.
Builds corrective and preventive action plans with owners, timelines, and success criteria.
Defines effectiveness verification approaches and closure criteria.
Outlines CAPA metrics, trend analysis, and management reporting content.
Maps CAPA activities to quality system and regulatory touchpoints.
limitations: Does not replace a qualified quality or regulatory professional.
Does not validate evidence or records inside a live quality system.
Referenced detailed guide files are listed but not included in this package.
The included script is only a placeholder and provides no CAPA automation.

use_cases.title: Plan a CAPA Investigation
use_cases.description: Create a structured investigation plan with scope, data needs, team roles, and root cause method selection.

use_cases.title: Prepare Corrective Action Plans
use_cases.description: Turn investigation findings into measurable actions with owners, due dates, deliverables, and verification criteria.

use_cases.title: Review CAPA System Performance
use_cases.description: Define metrics and trend summaries for overdue actions, recurrence, cycle time, and first-time effectiveness.

prompt_templates.title: Start a CAPA
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new CAPA structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me start a CAPA for this issue: [describe issue]. Include scope, initial containment, investigation questions, and required records.

prompt_templates.title: Choose RCA Method
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for investigation method selection.
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend the best root cause analysis method for this CAPA: [context]. Explain why and provide the investigation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Build Action Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for corrective and preventive action planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a corrective and preventive action plan from these root causes: [root causes]. Include owners, milestones, deliverables, risks, and verification criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Audit CAPA Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for inspection or management review preparation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this CAPA package for inspection readiness: [summary]. Identify documentation gaps, weak evidence, overdue risks, and closure concerns.

output_examples.input: A supplier defect repeated after two prior containment actions.
output_examples.output: The response would outline containment, supplier data collection, fishbone analysis, corrective actions, supplier follow-up, and recurrence checks.

output_examples.input: A CAPA is overdue and the owner says training is complete.
output_examples.output: The response would ask for training records, competency evidence, process performance data, and objective effectiveness criteria before closure.

output_examples.input: Quarterly management review needs CAPA metrics.
output_examples.output: The response would summarize cycle time, overdue rate, recurrence rate, first-time effectiveness, root cause themes, and escalation items.

best_practices: Provide factual issue details, dates, affected process areas, and known impact.
Include available evidence and state which facts are still unverified.
Ask for measurable effectiveness criteria before closing any CAPA.
anti_patterns: Do not treat training completion alone as proof of CAPA effectiveness.
Do not skip root cause verification when the apparent cause seems obvious.
Do not use the skill as final regulatory, legal, or quality approval.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure CAPA investigations, root cause analysis, action planning, effectiveness checks, and CAPA reporting.

faq.question: Can it close a CAPA for my organization?
faq.answer: No. It can prepare closure logic, but authorized quality personnel must approve closure.

faq.question: Does it include working automation scripts?
faq.answer: No. The included Python script is a placeholder and does not automate CAPA workflows.

faq.question: Which standards does it reference?
faq.answer: It references ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR 820.100, and EU MDR quality system concepts.

faq.question: Can it perform root cause analysis?
faq.answer: It can guide method selection and structure analysis. Users must provide accurate evidence and validate conclusions.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: The reviewed package has low risk. Static security hits were false positives, and the only real risk factor is a benign placeholder script.
